TEAM SITES
🌙
GameChanger strategy

GameChanger tracks the game. NextGen tells the team’s story.

From select softball experience, almost every serious team already uses GameChanger. It is the live scoring, streaming, stats, and game archive platform.

A parent usually mounts a phone on the backstop to stream the game, while another parent scores each pitch live. Because the scorebook and video are connected, families can go back later and jump directly to a specific at-bat, hit, inning, RBI, or play. GameChanger also has AI recaps, highlights, player stats, and archived games.

Not trying to replace GameChanger.
The better opportunity

GameChanger owns the live game. NextGen owns the branded team experience around the team.

NextGen becomes the clean public-facing home for a team.

🏠 Team website 🥎 Roster ⭐ Player profiles 📅 Schedule 🏆 Tournament links 💰 Sponsor placements 🎥 Media and highlights 🤝 Fundraising links 📲 GameChanger links 🎯 Recruiting visibility 🤖 RallyIQ content later
01

Who are the target customers?

The buyer is probably the team, coach, club, or parent-run organization.

The users are parents, family members, players, coaches, sponsors, and fans.

Game goers are part of the audience, but the bigger customer is the team that wants one polished place to send everyone.

02

What is the business model?

The MVP model is SaaS for teams and clubs.

A team pays monthly or annually for a branded site.

  • Starter team site
  • Pro team site with custom domain, sponsors, media, and player profiles
  • Club plan for organizations with multiple teams
  • Player/recruiting upgrades
  • Sponsor placement upgrades
  • AI recap/content upgrades through RallyIQ

The pitch is not “another team app.” It is a polished team presence and player visibility product.

03

How are we getting the game/player data?

For MVP, assume GameChanger is where most game data already lives.

But we should not depend on an unofficial GameChanger API as the core business foundation.

  1. Start with manual entry for roster, schedule, sponsors, player profiles, and links.
  2. Add CSV/stat import where possible.
  3. Link directly to GameChanger streams, archived games, and team pages.
  4. Prototype unofficial API access internally only, not as a promised customer feature.
  5. Later pursue official or partner integration if traction exists.
We integrate around where teams already live.
04

Does each customer get their own site?

Yes. Each team gets its own branded site, but all sites run on one shared multi-tenant platform.

Each tenant has its own branding, colors, logo, roster, schedule, sponsors, media, player profiles, and GameChanger links.

texasvenom.site atxprimetime.site jeremiah9980.github.io/Buzz-Elite-2035 jeremiah9980.github.io/bombers

Custom domains can come later.

One team example

Texas Venom shows the exact operations problem NextGen solves.

One coach, multiple teams, tournament systems, GameChanger, BAND, uniforms, schedules, field info, and constant parent questions. NextGen turns that communication chaos into one branded team hub.

→

Product roadmap

Phase 1

NextGen Team Site

Use the current nextgen-team-site UI as the branded front-end demo. Build the public team site experience first.

Phase 2

Team Admin OS

Add admin tools for roster, schedule, sponsors, announcements, media, player profiles, and links.

Phase 3

Game Data Layer

Allow imported stats, linked GameChanger games, player stat summaries, and season history.

Phase 4

RallyIQ

Add AI-generated player cards, game recaps, team summaries, sponsor posts, recruiting blurbs, and social content.

Phase 5

Rally OS

Expand into a broader operating system for clubs: multiple teams, tournaments, sponsors, fundraising, content automation, and analytics.

Simple positioning

NextGen Team Site is the modern branded home for youth sports teams.

RallyIQ is the intelligence layer that turns team data, game activity, stats, and media into player visibility, sponsor value, and automated storytelling.
